examples.title: Writing Pixel to Frame Buffer
examples.description: Basic algorithm for setting a pixel value in frame buffer
examples.explanation: Converts 2D pixel coordinates to 1D array index using row-major ordering, then packs RGBA components into a 32-bit integer for storage

examples.title: Z-Buffer Depth Test Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for depth testing and hidden surface removal
examples.code:
// Initialize Z-buffer with maximum depth
PROCEDURE InitializeBuffers(width, height)
BEGIN
 FOR y = 0 TO height-1 DO
 FOR x = 0 TO width-1 DO
 colorBuffer[x][y] = backgroundColor
 zBuffer[x][y] = MAX_DEPTH // Far plane value
 ENDFOR
 ENDFOR
END

// Process fragment with depth test
PROCEDURE DrawFragment(x, y, z, color)
BEGIN
 IF z < zBuffer[x][y] THEN
 // New fragment is closer - update both buffers
 zBuffer[x][y] = z
 colorBuffer[x][y] = color
 ENDIF
 // Else: fragment is hidden, discard
END
examples.explanation: Compares incoming fragment depth with stored value; only updates if fragment is closer to viewer (smaller z value)
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per fragment, O(wh) for full clear

examples.title: Double Buffer Swap
examples.description: Swapping front and back buffers for flicker-free rendering
examples.code:
// Global variables for double buffering
frontBuffer = pixelArray[0] // Currently displayed
backBuffer = pixelArray[1] // Being drawn to
bufferIndex = 0 // Tracks current front buffer

PROCEDURE RenderFrame()
BEGIN
 // Draw entire scene to back buffer
 ClearBuffer(backBuffer)
 DrawSceneTo(backBuffer)
 
 // Swap buffers when rendering complete
 SwapBuffers()
END

PROCEDURE SwapBuffers()
BEGIN
 // Atomic swap of buffer pointers
 bufferIndex = 1 - bufferIndex
 frontBuffer = pixelArray[bufferIndex]
 backBuffer = pixelArray[1 - bufferIndex]
 
 // Display hardware now automatically shows new frontBuffer
END
examples.explanation: Uses two buffers - GPU renders to back buffer while display shows front buffer; swapping is atomic operation, eliminating flicker
